rationale: Ground truth has no substantive concerns (just an lgtm), so both correctly approve with nothing to miss. X asserts unverifiable specifics as fact — a PR number (vllm#46022), an exact upstream line (instrumentator/basic.py:23), and a 'concrete repro' from the PR body — none of which are grounded in the given diff/ground-truth, reading as plausible fabrication. Y reaches the same correct verdict but explicitly labels its upstream-alignment claims as 'assumptions' rather than verified fact, and its per-hunk checklist stays tightly anchored to lines actually in the diff, making it more epistemically honest and grounded despite similar low actionability (nothing to action on an approve).
